description: The broad joint where arm meets torso, bearing loads and enabling the arm's full range. Shoulders define the body's width and carry its burdens—physical and metaphorical. They rise with tension, drop with release, and square with resolve. The shoulder is where responsibility is felt and burdens rest.
sound_symbolism: The word bears weight. 'Ta' begins with firm contact, bone meeting structure. 'Lua' opens broadly, spreading the load. The arc from solid stop to wide opening mirrors what shoulders do: anchor and extend.
grammatical_notes: Typically appears in pairs: 'wi talua' (two shoulders / both shoulders). Common in expressions of burden: 'talua lo phera' (the shoulder carries). Distinct from rokai (back), though they adjoin.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Shoulders know the weight of water carried, wood gathered, children held. Traditional work lives in shoulder memory, passed through generations of labor.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Shoulders express tension and ease non-verbally, communicating emotional state before words. Peace practice attends to shoulder release.

tags.physical: Upper torso joint bearing arms and loads
